$comment: Prompt set for the 2026-05-10 current-vs-slicing experiment (issue #71). 8 prompts across the four task modes #71 calls out (explain / debug / review / impact). Designed so each prompt has at least one obvious failure mode for naive retrieval (hub-dominated) and one obvious slicing win (cross-module behavior path).

prompts.text: Explain the authentication flow end to end, from the HTTP entry point to session persistence.
Trace the request path from the report controller to the final report persistence call. Include any queue, worker, or background job hops.
Why is report generation slow? Walk through the request path and call out the slowest hops, including any external service or LLM calls.
Where does the SESSION_TTL environment variable affect runtime behavior? Trace it from config load through every code path that reads it.
Can a PR that touches the onboarding flow break user creation, billing, or auth? Identify the call paths and risk hotspots.
Which controllers have an authentication guard but no authorization check? List the routes and the missing checks.
What can break if the research-agent service changes its public interface? List dependents, callers, and tests that would need to update.
When a user is charged, where are credits debited and how is the audit trail recorded? Identify all code paths that adjust the credit balance.
